text:
There once was a quiet town named Dewberry Hollow. The leaves rustled funny there. So everyone knew ol Pop Thornberry.

One autumn. You've gotta hear this. Ol Pop decided to harvest some moonshine. So he hid his booze in old oak bottles.

Then winter came. Birds ate the nuts. But no sap. So Pop froze.

Springtime brought thaw. Little critters snuck stuff.

Then Owl knew. Those bottles.

Owl ate berries.

Just one.

That oak?

Got him.

You think?

Now Owl owns that brew.

Haven't aged a second.

Just hops?

Like a moth.

You'd buy that?

I'd get Owl!

Maybe he'll brew us gin.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
